title: Today’s office look is proof that a white sundress can absolutely pull double duty. ☀️🤍
caption:
Today’s office look is proof that a white sundress can absolutely pull double duty. ☀️🤍

I paired a midi white button-front sundress with an orange floral scarf tied in a triangle at the waist, orange and tan raffia platform heels, a tan bag, and a chunky white sweater over my shoulders for those aggressively air-conditioned meeting rooms.

The best part? My orange nails accidentally became the hardest-working accessory in the outfit. At this point, I’m basically color-coordinated down to my fingertips. 🍊💅

This look is ready for a full day in the office and straight into dinner afterward, because if an outfit can survive spreadsheets and appetizers, it’s earned its place in the closet.

✨ Style tip: A colorful scarf tied at the waist is an easy way to define your silhouette and make a classic white dress feel fresh without adding extra layers.

 Xo,
Sus

title: Dress code: business… with a side of botanical garden. 🌿🌼
caption:
Dress code: business… with a side of botanical garden. 🌿🌼

Today’s office look is a green floral flounce-hem skirt paired with a white linen vest, an orange and green floral scarf, nude kitten heels, and a white bag. Because if my inbox is going to be chaotic, at least my outfit can look fresh and organized.

Style tip: A colorful scarf is one of the easiest ways to make a classic work outfit feel intentional. Tie it around your neck, bag, or waist for an instant personality boost.

Now if only my calendar looked this put together. 

Xo,
Sus

title: Sunday’s dress code: “cute aunt” with a side of poor temperature judgment. 😂❄️
caption:
Sunday’s dress code: “cute aunt” with a side of poor temperature judgment. 😂❄️

We spent the day celebrating my nephew’s 7th birthday at the ice rink, and while this outfit understood the assignment style-wise… it definitely did not understand the assignment temperature-wise. Turns out a light pink bodysuit and linen pants aren’t exactly built for Arctic conditions. At least the cream sweater over my shoulders looked adorable… even if it eventually had to be put to work. 🥶

I paired a light pink bodysuit with cream linen balloon-hem pants, a pink floral scarf tied around my waist, pink mesh flats, a cream sweater, and a pink bag for a soft, fun look that was perfect everywhere except inside the rink.

✨ Style tip: Tying a colorful scarf around your waist is an easy way to add personality, define your shape, and make a simple outfit feel instantly more polished. Just maybe pack an actual coat if you’re headed to an ice rink. 😉⛸️

Xo,
Sus

title: Back on U.S. soil just in time to trade French cafés for Fourth of July day drinks. 🇫🇷➡…
caption:
Back on U.S. soil just in time to trade French cafés for Fourth of July day drinks. 🇫🇷➡️🇺🇸❤️

Leaning into the holiday with this bright red floral lace mini dress, chunky raffia platforms, a neutral bag, and gold accessories. It’s equal parts festive and comfortable enough for a full day of celebrating. 🎇

Here’s to good friends, sunshine, and celebrating America’s birthday, even if my body is still on Paris time. 🍷✨

Style tip: A bold red dress does all the heavy lifting. Keep your shoes and bag neutral so the color stays the star of the outfit! 💃🏻

Xo,
Sus

title: Happy Pride event day at the office, which obviously called for maximum color. 🌈💖✨
caption:
Happy Pride event day at the office, which obviously called for maximum color. 🌈💖✨

Today’s outfit pairs a silky rainbow-hued maxi skirt with a chunky knit sleeveless turtleneck, plus pink sandal heels and a matching pink bag. Bright, cheerful, and proof that sometimes getting dressed is just an excuse to wear every happy color at once.

Style tip: A colorful statement skirt does all the heavy lifting: keep the top simple and pick one color from the skirt to repeat in your accessories for an effortlessly pulled-together look.

Xo,
Sus

title: In-office days are always a little more fun when the outfit feels polished but still co…
caption:
In-office days are always a little more fun when the outfit feels polished but still comfortable. 🤎❤️

Today’s look pairs a crisp white sleeveless button-down with a gold bow brooch at the neck, wide-leg chocolate brown leopard track pants, and a chocolate cashmere sweater draped over the shoulders for a chic touch. Finished with chocolate brown heels and a pop of red with my bag, because sometimes an office outfit deserves its own little plot twist.

Proof that track pants can, in fact, attend meetings when they’re dressed appropriately. 

✨ Style Tip: Mixing elevated basics with unexpected pieces (like sporty leopard pants and refined accessories) creates a look that feels both sophisticated and effortlessly modern. And never underestimate the power of a bold bag to bring everything together.

Xo,
Sus

title: Today’s look is proof that I clearly did not consult Coco Chanel before leaving the hou…
caption:
Today’s look is proof that I clearly did not consult Coco Chanel before leaving the house. While she famously said to take one accessory off before heading out… I apparently interpreted that as “add one more.”

A crisp white button-down dress with a collar set the stage, topped with a brooch at the neck, a colorful pink scarf tied triangle-style around the waist, and a pink sweater casually draped over the shoulders. Finished with pink pointy-toe shoes and a matching pink bag, this outfit is embracing the philosophy that more is more. 💗✨

Style tip: Accessories can completely transform a simple dress. Start with a classic base and layer in color, texture, and a few statement pieces to make the look uniquely yours.

Xo,
Sus

🔗 in bio

title: Today’s outfit is all about mixing sweet details with structured pieces. A boxy cropped…
caption:
Today’s outfit is all about mixing sweet details with structured pieces. A boxy cropped white button-down sweater keeps things crisp, while a slim blue floral mini skirt adds a touch of femininity. I echoed the print with a blue floral scarf tied at the neck and finished everything off with chunky white Mary Janes and sheer ruffled socks for a little vintage-inspired flair.

Style Tip: Pairing a boxy top with a more fitted skirt creates balance and helps define your shape while still keeping the overall look comfortable and effortless. ✨🌼

Xo,
Sus

🔗 in bio

title: Heading out to see @chiguy030 perform his long awaited solo at the ‘Cher The Love’ show…
caption:
Heading out to see @chiguy030 perform his long awaited solo at the ‘Cher The Love’ show tonight so it felt like the perfect occasion to lean into a look that’s just as joyful as the evening ahead! ✨

I paired a colorful cropped knit halter top with high-rise wide-leg white jeans for a polished but fun balance. Tan sandal heels keep the look light and summery, while a white belt with a gold buckle and a gold heart pendant necklace add a little extra shine. The finishing touch? A colorful beaded bag that ties all the vibrant tones together.

Can’t wait to cheer from the audience and celebrate an amazing performance tonight. 🎶❤️

Style Tip: When wearing a bold, colorful top, pair it with crisp white denim to let the colors stand out while keeping the overall look fresh, elevated, and easy to wear. ✨

Xo,
Sus

title: Matching the company color palette a little too well for today’s in-office day ❤️🌹
caption:
Matching the company color palette a little too well for today’s in-office day ❤️🌹

Went full red mode with a long sleeve bodysuit, pink and red floral skirt, red mesh kitten heels, gold jewelry, and a red lip.

From meetings to post-office dinner plans, this outfit understood the assignment.

Style tip: When wearing a bold color head-to-toe, mix textures and prints (like mesh, florals, and gold accents) to keep the look dimensional instead of flat.

Xo,
Sus

title: Nine years later and apparently I still believe the correct dress code for an anniversa…
caption:
Nine years later and apparently I still believe the correct dress code for an anniversary dinner is “romantic picnic blanket, but elevated.” 🌸💗

Wore a pink and white floral linen midi dress (yes, of course with pockets - is there any other way?!) with peasant detailing, added a pink cardigan over the shoulders for when the restaurant AC inevitably turns into arctic temperatures, plus a sparkly pink bag and tan raffia platforms to keep it feeling a little summery and fun.

Style tip: Raffia shoes are the easiest way to dress down a feminine floral dress just enough to make it feel effortless instead of overly formal.

Xo,
Sus

🔗 in bio

#summerdress #weddinganniversary #linenmididress #anniversarydinnerdate #summerstyleinspo

title: Channeling a little revolutionary chic for a day at Les Misérables 🎭💙
caption:
Channeling a little revolutionary chic for a day at Les Misérables 🎭💙

Tonight’s cast: a light blue and white striped button down layered under a matching corset, soft cream leather skirt, chocolate accessories, and a Chanel brooch at the neck because if I’m going to emotionally spiral during “I Dreamed a Dream,” I might as well do it accessorized.

Also yes… stripes again. At this point I’m either developing a signature look or slowly turning into a very polished mime. We may never know.

Styling tip: pairing soft blue tones with rich chocolate accessories keeps the look classic while adding warmth and depth, especially for plans that call for a little drama.

Xo,
Sus

title: Channeling coastal corporate chic for today’s in-office sailing theme ⚓️ A navy and whi…
caption:
Channeling coastal corporate chic for today’s in-office sailing theme ⚓️ A navy and white striped button down layered under a navy vest with seashell buttons felt perfectly nautical, especially paired with a silky navy mini skirt for a little movement and shine. I finished the look with white sheer polka dot socks and burgundy chunky Mary Janes for a playful touch that still felt office-ready.

Equal parts yacht club and Thursday meetings, which honestly might be my favorite dress code yet. ⛵️

Styling tip: mixing polished nautical staples with unexpected accessories (like sheer socks and chunky shoes) keeps a themed outfit feeling elevated instead of costume-y.

Xo, 
Sus

title: Channeling a little Elle Woods energy today because apparently pink really does solve e…
caption:
Channeling a little Elle Woods energy today because apparently pink really does solve everything 💕✨ Layered a pink and white striped collared button down under a matching pink vest, added a jeweled brooch at the neck for that extra polished detail, and paired it with a pink floral mini skirt that feels straight out of a modern-day Legally Blonde (or brunette!) moment. Finished it all off with pink pointy toe sling backs and a matching pink purse because commitment to the color story is key 💗🌸

Proof that being polished, feminine, and a little extra can absolutely coexist. Bend…and snap optional.

Xo,
Sus

title: Today’s office look was all about mixing polished classics with a little personality. A…
caption:
Today’s office look was all about mixing polished classics with a little personality. A crisp white button down paired with a blue and white striped blazer created the perfect tailored base, while the green silky floral skirt softened the look. The white and gold bow brooch added just the right amount of charm, and the chunky white Mary Janes kept the outfit feeling modern, playful, and comfortable enough for a full day of meetings.

A reminder that workwear can still feel creative, colorful, and very much like you ✨

Xo,
Sus

title: Turks & Caicos packing philosophy: the brighter the dress, the less likely I am to answ…
caption:
Turks & Caicos packing philosophy: the brighter the dress, the less likely I am to answer emails 🌴☀️

Look one is giving tropical spritz energy with a bright orange mini dress featuring a pleated bodice, flared skirt, and yes… pockets because vacation outfits deserve functionality too. Paired it with matching orange raffia heels and the most fun colorful beaded bag that feels like it came straight from a beach club gift shop in the best possible way.

Look two was made for sunset reservations by the water: a fitted halter mini covered in oversized florals in every shade of a Caribbean sunset. Styled with pink heels and a raffia + gold bag for that “I just threw this on after a beach day” illusion.

Vacation dressing should feel playful, a little dramatic, and ready for an extra round of cocktails by the ocean 🍹✨

Xo,
Sus

🔗 in bio 

#vacationoutfits #vacationoutfitideas #ootdvacation #ootdtravel #tropicalvacation

title: Office dressing, but make it feel elevated ✨ A sage green mesh bodysuit paired with a c…
caption:
Office dressing, but make it feel elevated ✨ A sage green mesh bodysuit paired with a cream leather midi skirt (with POCKETS), cognac belt and knee-high boots, finished with a tonal floral scarf tied at the neck for a little polished personality. Structured enough for meetings & comfortable enough for a full day at the office. 🤎🌿

This look really came together through the warm tonal palette and layered textures: soft mesh, sleek leather, rich cognac accessories, and a printed scarf to break everything up just enough.

Styling tip: when working with neutral outfits, add dimension by mixing textures and keeping accessories in the same color family. It makes the whole look feel intentional without trying too hard.

Xo,
Sus

title: Monochrome, but make it anything but boring 🤎✨
caption:
Monochrome, but make it anything but boring 🤎✨

Today’s lesson: taupe on taupe on taupe is the kind of quiet luxury that speaks volumes. A fuzzy wrap sweater bringing the cozy, a crepe cargo maxi skirt adding structure (because yes, we love fashion and functionality), finished with cream boots and a matching belt to keep it polished. Top it off with a tonal neck scarf for that “I absolutely planned this” energy.

Styling tip: Mixing textures in the same color family is the secret sauce: soft, structured, and sleek all in one look keeps monochrome feeling elevated instead of flat. Proof that neutrals can absolutely have a main character moment.

Xo,
Sus

🔗 in bio

title: This weekend’s fashion theme: one part polished, one part whimsy… with a strong commitm…
caption:
This weekend’s fashion theme: one part polished, one part whimsy… with a strong commitment to looking casually fabulous in mesh flats.

Look one was all about elevated ease: tan and cream floral barrel jeans paired with a dreamy peasant top, cream mesh flats, and layered gold necklaces. Feminine, effortless, and the perfect amount of floral for that Spring transition!

Look two was thrifted perfection: a pink and cream striped button down tied up just right, light denim, a cream and gold belt, pink mesh flats, and a pink floral scarf at the neck for that extra dose of personality. Playful, polished, and proof that the best outfits often start with a great thrift find.

Styling tip: When your outfit already has standout pieces, lean into coordinated accessories to tie it all together. Matching tones like cream, gold, and pink create that perfectly styled look without trying too hard… which is really the goal.

Xo,
Sus

🔗 in bio

#springoutfit #chicagostyle #floraldenim #meshflats #springstyle

title: Cream + chocolate might be the most underrated power couple in your closet 🤎✨
caption:
Cream + chocolate might be the most underrated power couple in your closet 🤎✨

This look is all about mixing textures while keeping the palette elevated and effortless. The structured cream denim vest top brings a tailored edge, balanced by the richness of a chocolate leather maxi skirt. Chocolate strappy heels keep everything sleek and streamlined, while layered gold necklaces add that warm glow moment we’re always chasing.

And the cream fur jacket? Draped, not worn, because obviously.

Styling tip: when you’re working within a neutral color story, lean into contrasting textures: denim, leather, and fur instantly make the outfit feel intentional and luxe without adding more color.

Xo,
Sus

🔗 in bio 

#neutralstyle #chicagostyle #texturemixing #elevatedbasics #leatherskirtoutfit

title: A little Parisian, a little dramatic, and fully committed to making officewear more int…
caption:
A little Parisian, a little dramatic, and fully committed to making officewear more interesting 💋✨

Today’s look pairs a white short-sleeved button-up sweater with gold buttons and a mixed-texture maxi skirt (structured leather up top, flowy chiffon pleats on the bottom) for that perfect balance of polished and movement. Finished with a black-and-white striped scarf tied at the neck, plus red heels and a matching red purse for the pop of color this outfit absolutely deserved.

Styling tip: When your outfit is built on neutrals, add one intentional bold accent color (like red) and repeat it in at least two places to make the whole look feel elevated and cohesive.

Au revoir, 
Sus

title: Spring forward for the office 🌿💙
caption:
Spring forward for the office 🌿💙

Nothing says “I survived winter and now have personality again” quite like a green tweed cropped blazer layered over a blue-and-white striped button down. Add a blue and green floral scarf for a little polished whimsy, then finish with great denim, cognac belt, and matching booties for that perfectly pulled-together spring office look.

Styling tip: Mixing stripes + florals works beautifully when they share a similar color palette. It keeps the look intentional, elevated, and just the right amount of fun.

Xo,
Sus

title: OOTD Round Up: Proof That Adding a Scarf Makes Every Outfit Better ✨🧣
caption:
OOTD Round Up: Proof That Adding a Scarf Makes Every Outfit Better ✨🧣

If an outfit feels like it’s missing something, the answer is almost always: add a scarf. Instantly more polished, more intentional, and approximately 73% more chic.

Look 1:
A navy tee + light wash jeans + tan suede belt gets an upgrade with a blue fur coat and patterned silk scarf for that “I just casually threw this on and somehow look expensive” effect.

Look 2:
A cashmere short sleeve knit and olive midi skirt become infinitely more interesting with cream knee-high boots and an orange/green scarf at the neck, aka the easiest way to make neutrals feel styled.

Look 3:
Canadian tuxedo, but make it cute: matching denim-on-denim layered with pink sneakers, a pink bag, and a pink/green silk scarf for the perfect playful finishing touch.

Styling Tip:
A silk scarf at the neck is the accessory equivalent of adding seasoning to food: small detail, huge impact.

Xo,
Sus

🔗 in bio

#chicagostyle #OOTDInspo #ScarfStyling #SilkScarfStyle #EverydayChic
snippet: OOTD Round Up: Proof That Adding a Scarf Makes Every Outfit Better ✨🧣 If an outfit feels like it’s missing something, the answer is almost always: add a scarf. Instantly more polished, more intentional, and approximately 73% more chic.

title: Wrigleyville during a Cubs game is the perfect excuse to blend game day spirit with a l…
caption:
Wrigleyville during a Cubs game is the perfect excuse to blend game day spirit with a little style ⚾️🍷

Today’s formula: a red tee as a subtle Cubs nod paired with favorite comfy Levi’s, because if I’m walking Wrigleyville for 6 hours, denim better have stretch and emotional support.

Elevated it with rich chocolate suede accessories (bomber, belt, and mules) for that polished-off-duty feel, then finished with a burgundy + red floral silk scarf at the neck because if there’s an opportunity for whimsy, I’m taking it.

Styling tip: Sports-event dressing doesn’t have to mean jersey-and-sneakers. Pull team colors into your outfit in a more elevated way and let accessories do the heavy lifting.

Xo,
Sus

title: Final day of the conference and we’re dressing like we still have one more outfit in us…
caption:
Final day of the conference and we’re dressing like we still have one more outfit in us ☕️

Nothing says “I networked for three days straight and still look polished” like a crisp cream and brown striped button down paired with cream denim: effortless, elevated, and just structured enough to convince people you still have energy. The chocolate suede belt and matching mules pull it all together in that rich, grounded way that feels intentional (even if you’re running on coffee and vibes).

And the real hero: an orange floral scarf tied in a triangle at the neck. It adds that perfect pop of personality and a little softness to the look. Like, yes I’m professional, but I also have range.

Styling tip: when your base is neutral-on-neutral, use texture (suede!) and a strategic pop of color to keep things interesting without overthinking it.

And to the man that asked if I always dress this eccentrically - yes, yes I do.

Xo,
Sus

🔗 in bio

#conferencestyle #outfitofthedayideas #styleinspiration #springstyles #creamdenim

title: Color therapy, but make it tailored 💥
caption:
Color therapy, but make it tailored 💥

A matching colorful skirt and blazer set is already doing the absolute most (in the best way), so keep the base clean with a crisp white sleeveless button-down. It balances the bold without dimming the fun: think “I’m polished” but also “I have a personality.”

Now for the details that make people stare a second longer: a pink brooch bow tie moment. It’s giving quirky, it’s giving intentional, it’s giving “yes, I planned this.” Let it sit right at the collar for maximum charm.

Sheer polka dot socks? Non-negotiable. They add that little wink of whimsy before you even get to the shoes. And the metallic loafers seal the deal: cool, reflective, and just a little bit unexpected.

Styling tip: when the outfit is this playful, commit fully. Half-measures don’t work here. Lean into the color, embrace the accessories, and let the whole look feel like a walking mood boost.

Xo,
Sus

title: Conference dressing, but make it quietly iconic 💼✨
caption:
Conference dressing, but make it quietly iconic 💼✨

A navy-on-navy base is clean, polished, and confident. The button-down paired with the satin skirt keeps things fluid and elevated, while the navy + plaid blazer adds just enough structure (and intrigue) to give that “I mean business, but I also have great taste” energy.

Now for the fun part: the details.
The designer-inspired scarf tied like a tie gives this look a smart, slightly playful twist: it’s polished, but not predictable.

And the cream moments? So good. The sheer polka dot ankle socks with the cream loafers soften the whole look and add a subtle pop that feels intentional, not loud.

✨ Styling tip: When you’re leaning into a darker monochrome palette, lighten things up with accessories and don’t shy away from mixing textures. It keeps the outfit from feeling flat and makes it look styled, not just put on.

Professional, pulled together, and just interesting enough to get compliments between sessions. 💁🏻‍♀️

Xo,
Sus

title: My tried and true travel outfit! ✈️
caption:
My tried and true travel outfit! ✈️

The formula: start with a Vuori matching set, aka the chicest way to feel like you’re in pajamas while looking like you have your life together. Layer on a classic trench (for drama… and unpredictable plane temps), then add crisp tennis shoes so you can sprint to your gate and look cute doing it.

Now for the fun: a matching pink suitcase because subtlety is overrated, a hat to say “I travel,” not “I survived,” and a sweater casually draped over your shoulders. Finish with gold jewelry to elevate the whole look from “comfy” to “who is she?”

Styling tip: Stick to a cohesive color palette (hello tonal queen) and let one piece (like that pink suitcase) be your main character moment. Travel days are long, but your outfit doesn’t have to suffer for it.

Xo,
Sus

title: When you find that great piece that fits perfectly, feels effortless, and somehow makes…
caption:
When you find that great piece that fits perfectly, feels effortless, and somehow makes every outfit better…you don’t hesitate. You get a second. Future you will be thrilled.

Look 1: Olive Monochrome
Go head-to-toe olive for that sleek, elongated effect. Pair leather pants with a matching top to keep things tonal, then layer in gold jewelry to add warmth and a little glow. A cream belt and coordinating booties break it up just enough so the look feels fresh, not flat. It’s polished and quietly confident.

Look 2: Leopard Barrel Jeans
These are the statement, so everything else can relax. Balance the shape with a more fitted or slightly cropped top to keep proportions in check. Finish with a structured pointed toe shoe to anchor the look. Think of leopard as your go-to neutral, just with a bit extra.

Moral of the story: when in doubt, double it. Your future self will thank you.

Xo,
Sus

title: 🐣🌸 Easter, but make it fashion 🌸🐣
caption:
🐣🌸 Easter, but make it fashion 🌸🐣

Look 1:
An all pink moment that says “I brought the deviled eggs and the personality.” Lean into the tonal magic - pink on pink adds just the right amount of whimsy without looking like you lost a fight with a peep. Style your cardigan two ways: wear it properly if there’s a breeze (responsible chic), or tie it over your shoulders for that “I summer in places with linen napkins” energy.

Look 2:
For the girls who like their Easter outfits with a twist - literally. Take your silk scarf, roll it up, and wear it as a belt around the waist of your dress. Unexpected? Yes. Compliment magnet? Also yes. Finish the look with your cardigan tied over your shoulders because we are nothing if not consistent (and slightly dramatic).

🐰 Bonus tip: If someone asks where your outfit is from, just say “thoughtfully curated” and hop away.

Xo,
Sus

title: The holy trinity of “small but mighty” accessories has entered the chat:
caption:
The holy trinity of “small but mighty” accessories has entered the chat:

Brooches:
Proof that your blazer doesn’t have to be boring. One brooch = instant main character energy. Pin it on a lapel, a sweater, or even your bag and suddenly you’re the most interesting person at brunch.
Tip: Don’t overthink placement - slightly off-center always looks more editorial than perfectly placed.

Socks:
The unsung hero of the outfit. A good sock peeking out says, “Yes, I meant to do this - and I’m a little more chic than you thought.”
Tip: Let them show on purpose - cropped pants + a sheer patterned sock makes the whole look feel styled and intentional.

Silk Scarves:
A silk scarf is less of an accessory and more of a personality trait. One minute it’s on your neck, the next it’s in your hair, on your bag, or tied like you summer in the South of France (even if you’re just going to Target).
Tip: Fold it into a thin triangle or band and tie it slightly loose at the neck - polished but never precious is the goal.

Moral of the story: it’s the little things… but make them fashion.

Xo,
Sus

🔗 in bio

#chicagostylists #springaccessories #springoutfitideas #springoutfitinspo #amazonaccessories

title: Hi! I’m Susanne 💖 I believe great style should feel effortless, personal, and bring out…
caption:
Hi! I’m Susanne 💖 I believe great style should feel effortless, personal, and bring out your most confident self! I love working closely with people to help them build wardrobes that reflect who they are and support how they live. My approach is thoughtful and collaborative - focused on ease, intention, and helping you feel polished without feeling overdone. 

I’d love to set up some time to chat with you and learn more about your wardrobe needs! Please feel free to send me a DM or fill out the link below to get started! 

https://docs.google.com/forms/d/e/1FAIpQLSe1j5IgJc4ijKbDBEsAQ7E-uDCSwxtz03nG2ZQc_UkaDgx3Yg/viewform 

Looking forward to meeting you!

Xo,
Sus

title: Who else has been in their scarf era lately? 🙋🏻‍♀️
caption:
Who else has been in their scarf era lately? 🙋🏻‍♀️
 
Lately I’ve been living in an early spring uniform: a monochromatic cream or white base, layered with chocolate brown accessories and finished with a little scarf moment at the neck. It’s equal parts “hello, spring” and “yes, I do have my life together” (even if I don’t!). 🌻

Xo,
Sus
